title: Strategy and Grand Strategy
gist: IISS landing page for Joshua Rovner's Adelphi book distinguishing strategy (a theory of victory) from grand strategy (a theory of security), and arguing that misaligning the two has historically led to national ruin amid great-power competition. Mostly promotional book copy and blurbs, not analytical content.

insights.claim: Joshua Rovner frames strategy as 'a theory of victory' and grand strategy as 'a theory of security,' arguing leaders routinely conflate the two and that the misalignment can lead to national ruin.
insights.speaker: IISS
insights.so_what: A reusable mental model for assessing whether a state's wartime conduct (e.g. Russia, Israel) serves its post-war security or just battlefield wins — relevant to pricing conflict-duration and escalation risk.
insights.quote: Rovner offers a lucid analysis of strategy (a theory of victory) and grand strategy (a theory of security).
insights.locator: In this Adelphi book, Joshua Rovner offers

insights.claim: IISS positions the strategy/grand-strategy distinction as newly urgent because the world is entering 'a new era of great power competition' alongside extraordinary technological change.
insights.speaker: IISS
insights.so_what: Signals the defense-intellectual establishment's framing of the current cycle as structurally competitive — supportive of sustained defense-spending and deterrence narratives.
insights.quote: as we enter a new era of great power competition
